food_subgroup: Jellyfish and marine invertebrates — cold salad preparation

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general jellyfish food product class (FOODON_03311737); no specific entry for cold jellyfish salad with green vegetables. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers jellyfish as a food commodity. No specific prepared cold salad entry exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for prepared jellyfish salad dish.

preparation_method: seasoned with sesame oil and vinegar

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of jellyfish salad with jade-green vegetables is approximately 200 g, typically presented as a cold appetiser plate.

nutrient_source.title: Jellyfish, dried, salted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15244) and peer-reviewed compositional data for prepared cold jellyfish salad
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / peer-reviewed literature (Nishimoto et al., 2012; FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 翡翠凉丝海蜇 (cold jellyfish salad with jade-green vegetables) exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for prepared jellyfish (NDB 15244: ~11 kcal, 2.6 g protein, 0.1 g fat, 0.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g) combined with compositional adjustments for the green vegetable and seasoning components (cucumber, spinach, sesame oil, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic) based on FAO/INFOODS SEA ingredient data. The final blended per-100 g estimate reflects a typical restaurant-style preparation ratio of approximately 60% jellyfish, 25% green vegetables, 15% dressing/seasoning.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed seafood-vegetable cold dishes — analogous estimate based on jellyfish (very low carbohydrate food) and non-starchy vegetable components; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cold jellyfish salad. Jellyfish is a very low-carbohydrate marine invertebrate (approximately 0.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g raw). The dish contains non-starchy vegetables (cucumber, spinach) and a small amount of sugar in the dressing. GI is estimated at 15 (low) by analogy with other non-starchy vegetable and seafood cold dishes documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (15 × 4.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 0.63, rounded to 0.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from desalted jellyfish and soy sauce seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 200 g serving provides approximately 840 mg sodium (35% of the 2400 mg daily limit), and should request reduced soy sauce or consume smaller portions to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 2.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g and an estimated GI of 15, this dish has a negligible glycemic load and is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as part of a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium from processing and seasoning; low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat (0.3 g per 100 g) and contains beneficial unsaturated fats from sesame oil, but the high sodium content warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease who are managing blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jellyfish Salad with Jade-green Vegetables (翡翠凉丝海蜇, fěicuì liáng sī hǎizhé) is a classic Chinese cold appetiser featuring processed, desalted jellyfish served alongside jade-green vegetables such as cucumber and spinach or other leafy greens. The dish is dressed with a seasoning blend of sesame oil, rice vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, and chilli oil, producing a flavour profile that is savoury, crisp, lightly tangy, and mildly spicy. The jellyfish provides a distinctive crunchy, gelatinous texture that is prized in Chinese cuisine. The name 翡翠 (fěicuì, meaning jade or emerald) refers to the vivid green colour of the vegetable accompaniment. This dish is commonly served as a cold starter in Chinese restaurants across Singapore and mainland China. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie, low-carbohydrate dish with moderate protein from the jellyfish and a notable sodium content due to the desalting process and soy-based dressing. The estimated glycemic index is low (approximately 15), making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. The primary clinical concern is sodium, which may be elevated for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is dairy-free and contains soy and sesame allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy jellyfish data combined with FAO/INFOODS SEA ingredient composition data for the vegetable and dressing components. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the primary ingredient (jellyfish) has well-documented compositional data and the dish components are well-characterised.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy (NDB 15244, prepared jellyfish) and FAO/INFOODS SEA ingredient data for a blended preparation of approximately 60% jellyfish, 25% green vegetables, 15% dressing. Sodium is elevated due to residual salt from jellyfish processing and soy sauce in the dressing; actual sodium may vary significantly by restaurant preparation. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this preparation. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as jellyfish and vegetable ingredients contain no industrially produced trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy data. GI is estimated at 15 (low) by analogy with non-starchy vegetable and seafood cold dishes per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. The dish is served at See Ya Tonight 今晚有局 (Singapore) on field survey at SGD 16.64. Halal status is unconfirmed as jellyfish processing and seasoning sources are not verified.
